CREATE TABLE article (id INT, description TEXT, pub_date DATE, author_name VARCHAR(100));
CREATE TABLE doc (id INT, content TEXT);
CREATE TEXT INDEX ON article (description) WITH KEY dtid CLUSTERED WITH (pub_date, author_name) USING FUNCTION article_description_index_hook;
INSERT INTO article (id, description, pub_date, author_name) VALUES (1, 'A study on free text indexing.', '2023-01-01', 'Alice');
INSERT INTO article (id, description, pub_date, author_name) VALUES (2, 'An overview of full-text search.', '2023-01-02', 'Bob');
INSERT INTO doc (id, content) VALUES (1, '<text>This is a sample XML content for indexing.</text>');
INSERT INTO doc (id, content) VALUES (2, '<text>Another piece of content for text indexing example.</text>');
SELECT * FROM article WHERE CONTAINS(description, 'study') AND pub_date > '2022-12-31';
SELECT a.id, a.description FROM article a JOIN doc d ON CONTAINS(a.description, 'index') WHERE CONTAINS(d.content, 'indexing');
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM article WHERE CONTAINS(description, 'overview') AND author_name = 'Bob';
CREATE TEXT INDEX ON article (description) WITH KEY dtid CLUSTERED WITH (pub_date) USING FUNCTION article_hook;
SELECT d.id FROM doc d WHERE CONTAINS(content, 'content') OR d.id IN (SELECT a.id FROM article a WHERE CONTAINS(a.description, 'text'));
DROP INDEX IF EXISTS dtid ON article;
SELECT DISTINCT pub_date FROM article WHERE CONTAINS(description, 'free text');
DELETE FROM doc WHERE id IN (SELECT d.id FROM doc d WHERE NOT CONTAINS(content, 'XML'));
